maxghenis.com · v2 · last built Apr 21, 2026An instrument for simulating society

Building democratic infrastructure for simulating society.

I build microsimulation models and the tools around them — PolicyEngine, Cosilico, applied papers, and the agent-era infrastructure for turning statutes, surveys, and scientific evidence into software policy-makers and households can actually use. Everything here is open source.

0
Jurisdictions simulated
0
Reform scenarios run
0
Open repos published
0
Lines of Python
§ 01·Microsimulations, papers, and live calculators.

Models

Index →
  • What Nut?paper · live · 2026

    Skeptical Monte Carlo evidence synthesis of mortality benefit from daily nut consumption, 8 nut types, 10k draws.

    →
  • PolicyEnginefounder · 2021 →

    Open microsimulation for US, UK, Canada. Reform scoring, distributional impact, personal calculator.

    ↗
§ 02·Infrastructure for working with agents and code.

Tools

Index →
  • Straudecli · typescript

    Strava for AI coding. Track tokens, spend, and streaks across Claude Code and Codex. Global leaderboard.

    ↗
  • Terminal Gridextension · typescript

    VS Code extension for keyboard-driven terminal grid management with auto-launch for AI coding tools.

    ↗
§ 03·Essays, notes, and the Society in Silico book.

Writing

Index →
  • Society in Silicobook · in progress

    A book on the history of microsimulation — from Orcutt's 1957 vision to democratic policy infrastructure in the agent era.

    →
§ 04·Personal and applied open datasets.

Data

Index →
  • Agent usagedaily · updated nightly

    Daily Claude Code + Codex usage aggregates. Live dashboard of tokens, sessions, spend.

    →
